examples: The boy was morbidly sensitive, with a hallucinatory imagination.
The novel's hallucinatory world is evoked through film projections, puppets and music.
A style using subconscious mental activity as its subject matter, characterized by dreamlike, hallucinatory imagery.
Because foreign travel is at times almost hallucinatory, you need to record everything, as well as your own disbelief.
There's something hallucinatory about climbing these mountains - drivers on one road have talked of sensing an extra passenger in the back seat.
